toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run the tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

To collect crash dumps on .NET, I can use the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable (or `COMPlus_DbgEnableMiniDump` for older runtimes). For .NET 9, the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` variable is the way to go.

The relevant environment variables are:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory). Options: 1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` — path/pattern for the dump file

Now I'll set these environment variables and run the tests.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-fe5c3a7aabd64376891b300aa580e336 && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"/tmp/sv-fe5c3a7aabd64376891b300aa580e336/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\n  dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-fe5c3a7aabd64376891b300aa580e336 && \
  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="/tmp/sv-fe5c3a7aabd64376891b300aa580e336/crashdump_%p.dmp" \
  dotnet test -v normal 2>&1

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with verbose output

data.reasoningText: Hmm, the tests ran but I don't see normal test output. Let me try without `--no-restore` and see the full output. Actually wait, it does show "Build succeeded" but it doesn't show the test results. The `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` might be causing this. Let me try with verbosity.
